layman_headline: Crypto market stuck in fear mode — no clean entry worth the risk right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dominance high at 56.3%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins (altcoins) and into Bitcoin. All of the potential trade candidates are either in downtrends, have already bounced too far too fast, or lack clear chart confirmation for a reversal. While a few coins like ZRO and TRUMP are near oversold levels, their charts are still bearish with no sign of buyers stepping in convincingly. It's better to wait for a clearer setup than to force a trade in these conditions.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. Don't chase anything here. If ZRO holds the $0.90 support and forms a bullish reversal candle in the next 1-2 days, that could become actionable. Otherwise, patience is the best trade right now.
expert_reasoning: Reviewing all 9 swing candidates, the environment is hostile for altcoin longs: BTC dominance at 56.3% is compressing alt valuations, Fear & Greed at 26 is improving but still fearful, and most candidates show bearish chart structures. TRUMP and ZRO have the best RSI readings (29 and 31 respectively — historically reliable bounce zones per our signal credibility data showing RSI with 75% win rate). However, both are below all major moving averages with bearish chart bias scores of -0.70. ARB's +25% weekly gain makes it overextended. JTO's RSI at 59 suggests it's already mid-move. Without a volume breakout signal (our most reliable indicator at 100% win rate) or a confirming news catalyst from CoinTelegraph (also 100% win rate), entering here lacks the edge our historical data demands. The CoinTelegraph headline about Bitcoin being in 'late stages of bear market' is notable but not actionable for altcoin entries yet.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.3% continues to crush altcoin relative performance — any further BTC dominance expansion would drag all candidates lower
No volume breakout signals detected on any candidate, which is our most reliable entry confirmation
Fear & Greed at 26 could deteriorate further if BTC loses the $63K level, triggering another leg down in alts
watch_list: ZRO holding $0.90 support with a bullish reversal candle (hammer or morning star) in the next 48 hours
Bitcoin price action around $63K-$64K — a breakout above $65K could ease BTC dominance and help alts
Fear & Greed index — a move above 30 would confirm the improving trend and make alt entries safer
market_context: The crypto market is in a fearful but slowly improving state, with BTC holding near $63.8K and dominance at a cycle high of 56.3%. Altcoins are broadly suffering with most candidates down 10-25% on the week, but no clear capitulation bottom or reversal confirmation is visible yet.
context_banner: High BTC dominance and fearful sentiment make this a patience trade — the best setup is no setup until confirmation arrives.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 ('Fear') with an improving trend from week average of 24 — historically an 80% reliable signal but not yet at extreme levels that trigger strongest bounces.
ZRO RSI at 31 and TRUMP RSI at 29 are near oversold territory — historically a 75% reliable bounce signal, but both lack chart confirmation to act on.
CoinTelegraph reports Bitcoin may be in 'late stages of bear market' per Jamie Coutts — a macro-bullish narrative but not yet confirmed by price action or altcoin recovery.
No volume breakout detected on any candidate — our most reliable signal (100% win rate) is absent, which is a strong reason to wait.
BIP 110 fork deadline nearing with zero miner support and Empery Digital selling half its BTC stack create headline uncertainty that suppresses risk appetite.
